title: First Unique Character
description:
Given a string `s`, find the index of the **first non-repeating character**. If no such character exists, return `-1`.

This is critical in real-time systems — e.g. processing a stream of event codes where the first anomalous (non-duplicate) event must be flagged immediately.

**Constraints:**
- 1 <= s.length <= 10^5
- `s` consists of lowercase English letters only

testCases.description: First unique char is 'x' at index 2
All characters repeat — no unique char
Unique char near the end of a long repeating pattern

hints: Make two passes: first count every character's frequency, then find the first with count 1.
A hash map (or fixed-size array of 26 for lowercase letters) works perfectly for counting.
Don't forget — the answer is the *index* of the character, not the character itself.

complexity.explanation: Two linear passes over the string; the hash map stores at most 26 entries (lowercase letters), so space is constant.
